mnemonics: ANOVA - Analyze Variance Over All Groups. Remember: Always Note Overarching Variance Averages.
keyPoints: ANOVA is used to compare the means of three or more groups to see if at least one is statistically different.
It partitions the total variance into between-group variance (treatment effect) and within-group variance (error).
The F-statistic is calculated as the ratio of between-group mean square to within-group mean square.
commonMistakes: Using multiple t-tests instead of ANOVA, which increases the chance of a Type I error (false positive).
Failing to check the assumptions of ANOVA: normality, homogeneity of variances, and independence of observations.
